primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of an intragroup dividend receivable for foreign‑exchange hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Limits hedge accounting to assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with a party external to the reporting entity; intragroup transactions are excluded from consolidated statements.
Clarifies that a net‑investment hedge can be applied to the foreign‑currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation and defines the eligible hedged risk.

Provides an exception: the foreign‑currency risk of an intragroup monetary item may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if it results in an exposure to exchange gains or losses that is not fully eliminated on consolidation.

supporting_authority.reason: Governs recognition of exchange differences on monetary items and explains when intragroup monetary items create exposures that are not eliminated on consolidation.
Describes the criteria for designating a hedging relationship and assessing effectiveness, relevant to evaluating whether the intragroup dividend receivable could meet those requirements.

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses cash‑flow classification but does not affect the eligibility of intragroup items for hedge accounting.
Covers non‑reciprocal dividend distributions; not relevant to hedge‑accounting eligibility.
Deals with related‑party disclosures; does not govern hedge‑accounting treatment.
Addresses tax‑status changes; not relevant to foreign‑exchange hedge accounting for intragroup dividends.

treatment_families.family: Intragroup items not eligible for hedge accounting in consolidated statements (absent a specific exception)
Net‑investment hedge for foreign‑currency risk when the intragroup receivable is part of a net investment in a foreign operation
Investment‑entity exception allowing hedge accounting for intragroup transactions with subsidiaries measured at fair value through profit or loss

approaches.label: Intragroup dividend receivable not eligible for hedge accounting in consolidated statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedge accounting to external transactions; intragroup dividend receivables are eliminated in consolidation and cannot be designated as hedged items unless a specific exception applies.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge may be applied if the dividend receivable is part of the net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 and IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 permit a hedge of the foreign‑currency risk of an intragroup monetary item that forms part of a net investment in a foreign operation, provided the risk affects consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Investment‑entity exception may allow hedge accounting for intragroup dividend receivable with subsidiaries measured at fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 contains an exception for investment entities (as defined in IFRS 10) where intragroup transactions with subsidiaries measured at fair value through profit or loss are not eliminated and may be hedged.
